compiled_state.title: Worktree isolation auth marker not recognized by pre-agent hook
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description: When the sprint orchestrator writes /tmp/worktree-isolation-authorized-<uuid> with the claude process PID (49858) before dispatching a sub-agent with isolation: worktree, the pre-agent hook still blocks with 'Worktree isolation is disabled for sub-agents'. The hook uses kill -0 to verify PID liveness. Verified: kill -0 49858 returns 0 (alive) when checked via Bash tool. The auth marker file exists at dispatch time. Expected: hook allows the dispatch. Actual: hook denies regardless of marker file.

comments.body: Investigation: hook_worktree_isolation_guard correctly allows dispatch when a valid auth marker exists — verified via unit test (test_function_allows_with_valid_marker passes). The deny path only fires when isolation_enabled=true AND no live-PID marker exists. The bug may have been observed during a transient state (e.g., config not yet set, or marker written with subprocess PID that exited before hook checked). Current code is correct. Recommend closing after user confirmation.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Escalation: unit test test_function_allows_with_valid_marker passes — hook code is correct. The bug was reported during a live sprint session and may have been transient (e.g., PID written before claude subprocess forked). No code fix identified. Escalating to user for close confirmation or additional reproduction steps.
comments.author: Test
